issue.message: Call requires permission which may be rejected by user: code should explicitly check to see if permission is available (with `checkPermission`) or explicitly handle a potential `SecurityException`
Call requires permission which may be rejected by user: code should explicitly check to see if permission is available (with `checkPermission`) or explicitly handle a potential `SecurityException`

Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
issue.errorLine1: text = String.format("%02d:%02d / %02d:%02d", mm, ss, maxMm, maxSs),

issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead

Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
The locale folder "`he`" should be called "`iw`" instead; see the `java.util.Locale` documentation
The locale folder "`id`" should be called "`in`" instead; see the `java.util.Locale` documentation
Field requires API level 29 (current min is 26): `android.provider.MediaStore.Downloads#EXTERNAL_CONTENT_URI`
Not targeting the latest versions of Android; compatibility modes apply. Consider testing and updating this version. Consult the `android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES` javadoc for details.
Not targeting the latest versions of Android; compatibility modes apply. Consider testing and updating this version. Consult the `android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES` javadoc for details.
Windows file separators (`\`) and drive letter separators (':') must be escaped (`\\`) in property files; use C\\:\\\\Users\\\\Toha\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\Android\\\\Sdk\\r

issue.message: Suspicious indentation: This is indented but is not continuing the previous expression (`val result = buf.arr`...)
The indentation string here is different from on the previous line (`" "` vs `\t`)
Suspicious indentation: This is indented but is not continuing the previous expression (`val encoded = packet`...)
Suspicious indentation: This is indented but is not continuing the previous expression (`val shouldSendReadRe`...)

Use of `REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS` violates the Play Store Content Policy regarding acceptable use cases, as described in https://developer.android.com/training/monitoring-device-state/doze-standby.html

`forceFinishReceiver` is missing `RECEIVER_EXPORTED` or `RECEIVER_NOT_EXPORTED` flag for unprotected broadcasts registered for com.echo.android.ACTION_FORCE_FINISH

Optional Modifier parameter should have a default value of `Modifier`
Modifier parameter should be the first optional parameter

Don't use a constant here; expected one of: `ScanCallback.SCAN_FAILED_ALREADY_STARTED`, `ScanCallback.SCAN_FAILED_APPLICATION_REGISTRATION_FAILED`, `ScanCallback.SCAN_FAILED_FEATURE_UNSUPPORTED`, `ScanCallback.SCAN_FAILED_INTERNAL_ERROR`, `ScanCallback.SCAN_FAILED_OUT_OF_HARDWARE_RESOURCES`, `ScanCallback.SCAN_FAILED_SCANNING_TOO_FREQUENTLY`
For locale "he" (Hebrew) the following quantity should also be defined: `two`
For locale "pl" (Polish) the following quantities should also be defined: `few` (e.g. "2 miesiące"), `many` (e.g. "5 miesięcy")
For locale "ru" (Russian) the following quantities should also be defined: `few` (e.g. "из 2 книг за 2 дня"), `many` (e.g. "из 5 книг за 5 дней")
For locale "ru" (Russian) the following quantities should also be defined: `few` (e.g. "из 2 книг за 2 дня"), `many` (e.g. "из 5 книг за 5 дней")
For locale "ar" (Arabic) the following quantities should also be defined: `few` (e.g. "3"), `many` (e.g. "11"), `two` (e.g. "2"), `zero` (e.g. "0")
For locale "es" (Spanish) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`
For locale "pt" (Portuguese) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`
For locale "pt" (Portuguese) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`

For locale "pt" (Portuguese) the following quantity should also be defined: `many`
For locale "fr" (French) the following quantity should also be defined: `many` (e.g. "1000000 de jours")
For locale "fr" (French) the following quantity should also be defined: `many` (e.g. "1000000 de jours")

issue.errorLine1: <string name="permission_bluetooth_rationale">Bluetooth permission is required for peer-to-peer messaging without internet.</string>
<string name="permission_location_rationale">Location permission is required to discover nearby devices via Bluetooth.</string>
<string name="permission_notification_rationale">Notification permission is required to alert you of new messages.</string>
<string name="emergency_clear_hint">Triple tap to clear all data</string>

<string name="battery_optimization_explanation">Echo runs in the background to maintain mesh network connections with nearby devices. Battery optimization can interrupt these connections, causing messages to be delayed or missed.\n\nDisabling battery optimization ensures reliable peer-to-peer messaging.</string>
<string name="battery_optimization_note">Note: You can change this setting later in Android Settings > Apps > Echo > Battery</string>
<string name="battery_optimization_not_supported_explanation">Your device doesn\'t require battery optimization settings. Echo will run normally.</string>
<string name="battery_optimization_not_supported_message">Your device doesn\'t require battery optimization settings. Echo will run normally.</string>
<string name="battery_optimization_success_message">Echo can run reliably in the background</string>

<string name="battery_optimization_benefits">• Ensures reliable message delivery
• Maintains mesh network connectivity
• Allows background message relay
• Prevents connection drops</string>
<string name="mesh_service_channel_desc">Keeps the Bluetooth mesh running in the background</string>
<string name="about_tagline">decentralized mesh messaging with end-to-end encryption</string>
<string name="about_offline_mesh_desc">Communicate directly via Bluetooth LE without internet or servers. Messages relay through nearby devices to extend range.</string>
<string name="about_online_geohash_desc">Connect with people in your area using geohash-based channels. Extend the mesh using public internet relays.</string>
<string name="about_e2e_desc">Private messages are encrypted. Channel messages are public.</string>
<string name="about_pow_tip">add proof of work to geohash messages for spam deterrence.</string>
<string name="about_tor_route">route internet over tor for enhanced privacy.</string>
<string name="about_emergency_tip">Tip: Triple-click the app title to emergency delete all stored data including messages, keys, and settings.</string>
<string name="about_background_desc">keep mesh active when app is closed (foreground service)</string>
<string name="choose_action_message_or_user">choose an action for this message or user</string>
<string name="choose_action_user">choose an action for this user</string>
<string name="action_block_subtitle">block all messages from this user</string>

<string name="location_channels_desc">chat with people near you using geohash channels. only a coarse geohash is shared, never exact gps. do not screenshot or share this screen to protect your privacy.</string>
<string name="location_permission_denied">location permission denied. enable in settings to use location channels.</string>
<string name="location_notes_description">add short permanent notes to this location for other visitors to find.</string>
<string name="location_notes_no_relays_desc">notes rely on geo relays. check connection and try again.</string>
<string name="location_notes_empty_desc">be the first to add one for this spot.</string>
<string name="debug_tools_desc">developer utilities for diagnostics and control</string>
<string name="debug_verbose_hint">logs peer joins/leaves, connection direction, packet routing and relays</string>
<string name="debug_roles_hint">turn roles on/off and close all connections when disabled</string>
<string name="location_explanation">Echo does NOT track your location.\n\nLocation services are required for Bluetooth scanning and for the Geohash chat feature.</string>

<string name="location_needs_bullets">• Bluetooth device scanning
• Discovering nearby users on mesh network
• Geohash chat feature
• No tracking or location collection</string>
<string name="background_location_explanation">Android recommends background location so Echo can scan for nearby devices while the app is not open. This keeps the mesh alive after reboot.</string>
<string name="background_location_settings_tip">When settings opens, choose "Allow all the time".</string>
<string name="background_location_needs_bullets">- scan for nearby devices while the app is closed\n- reconnect after reboot\n- keep the mesh running in the background</string>
<string name="background_location_privacy_note">We NEVER collect or store your location. Your privacy is safe.</string>

<string name="location_unavailable_explanation">Location services are not available on this device. This is unusual as location services are standard on Android devices.\n\nEcho needs location services for Bluetooth scanning to work properly (Android requirement). Without this, the app cannot discover nearby users.</string>

<string name="bluetooth_needs_bullets">• Discover nearby users
• Create mesh network connections
• Send and receive messages
• Work without internet or servers</string>

<string name="bluetooth_unsupported_explanation">This device doesn\'t support Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), which is required for Echo to function.\n\nEcho needs BLE to create mesh networks and communicate with nearby devices without internet.</string>
<string name="battery_optimization_explanation_short">Echo needs to run in the background to maintain mesh connections. battery optimization can interrupt these connections.</string>
<string name="battery_benefits_short">• reliable message delivery\n• maintains mesh connectivity\n• prevents connection drops</string>
<string name="should_take_seconds">This should only take a few seconds</string>
<string name="privacy_bullets">• no tracking or data collection\n• Bluetooth mesh chats are fully offline\n• Geohash chats use the internet</string>
<string name="location_tracking_warning">Echo does NOT track your location</string>
<string name="pan_zoom_instruction">pan and zoom to select a geohash</string>
<string name="perm_nearby_devices_desc">Required to discover Echo users via Bluetooth</string>
<string name="perm_nearby_devices_system">Allow Echo to connect to nearby devices</string>
<string name="perm_location_desc">Required by Android to discover nearby Echo users via Bluetooth</string>
<string name="perm_location_system">Echo needs this to scan for nearby devices</string>
<string name="perm_background_location_desc">Recommended to scan for nearby devices while the app is in the background</string>
<string name="perm_notifications_desc">Receive notifications when you receive private messages</string>
<string name="perm_notifications_system">Allow Echo to send you notifications</string>
<string name="perm_battery_desc">Disable battery optimization to ensure Echo runs reliably in the background and maintains mesh network connections</string>
<string name="perm_battery_system">Allow Echo to run without battery restrictions</string>
<string name="pwd_prompt_message">Channel %1$s is password protected. Enter the password to join.</string>

issue.message: The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (1, 21, 31, 41, 51, 61, 71, 81, 101, 1001, …), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (0, 1), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (0, 1), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (0, 1), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (0, 1), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (0, 1), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (0, 1), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (0, 1), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

The quantity `'one'` matches more than one specific number in this locale (0, 1), but the message did not include a formatting argument (such as `%d`). This is usually an internationalization error. See full issue explanation for more.

Using `getAddress` to get device identifiers is not recommended
issue.errorLine1: fun getLocalAdapterAddress(): String? = try { bluetoothAdapter?.address } catch (e: Exception) { null }

issue.message: Using `getAddress` to get device identifiers is not recommended
Using `getAddress` to get device identifiers is not recommended

This broadcast receiver declares an intent-filter for a protected broadcast action string, which can only be sent by the system, not third-party applications. However, the receiver's `onReceive` method does not appear to call `getAction` to ensure that the received Intent's action string matches the expected value, potentially making it possible for another actor to send a spoofed intent with no action string or a different action string and cause undesired behavior.
issue.errorLine1: override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {

issue.message: Unnecessary; `Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O` is always true here (`SDK_INT` ≥ 26 and < 31)
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `FavoritesPersistenceService` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `GeohashBookmarksStore` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `LocationChannelManager` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `BluetoothMeshService` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `MessageRouter` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `NostrClient` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `NostrTransport` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `SeenMessageStore` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
This initialization lambda (`{ 0 }`) is unnecessary and is less efficient

issue.message: This initialization lambda (`{ 0x00.toByte() }`) is unnecessary and is less efficient
This initialization lambda (`{ 0f }`) is unnecessary and is less efficient
This initialization lambda (`{ 0f }`) is unnecessary and is less efficient
This initialization lambda (`{ 0 }`) is unnecessary and is less efficient

issue.errorLine1: var selectedTab by remember { mutableStateOf(0) } // 0 = My Code, 1 = Scan

issue.message: The resource `R.string.permission_location_rationale` appears to be unused
issue.errorLine1: <string name="permission_location_rationale">Location permission is required to discover nearby devices via Bluetooth.</string>

issue.message: The resource `R.string.permission_notification_rationale` appears to be unused
issue.errorLine1: <string name="permission_notification_rationale">Notification permission is required to alert you of new messages.</string>

issue.message: The resource `R.string.battery_optimization_detected` appears to be unused
The resource `R.string.battery_optimization_disabled` appears to be unused
The resource `R.string.battery_optimization_checking` appears to be unused
The resource `R.string.battery_optimization_why_disable` appears to be unused
The resource `R.string.battery_optimization_explanation` appears to be unused
issue.errorLine1: <string name="battery_optimization_explanation">Echo runs in the background to maintain mesh network connections with nearby devices. Battery optimization can interrupt these connections, causing messages to be delayed or missed.\n\nDisabling battery optimization ensures reliable peer-to-peer messaging.</string>

issue.message: The resource `R.string.battery_optimization_disable_button` appears to be unused
The resource `R.string.battery_optimization_note` appears to be unused
issue.errorLine1: <string name="battery_optimization_note">Note: You can change this setting later in Android Settings > Apps > Echo > Battery</string>

issue.message: The resource `R.string.battery_optimization_not_supported_explanation` appears to be unused
issue.errorLine1: <string name="battery_optimization_not_supported_explanation">Your device doesn\'t require battery optimization settings. Echo will run normally.</string>

issue.message: The resource `R.string.battery_optimization_benefits` appears to be unused
issue.errorLine1:
<string name="battery_optimization_benefits">• Ensures reliable message delivery
• Maintains mesh network connectivity
• Allows background message relay
• Prevents connection drops</string>

Launcher icon used as round icon did not have a circular shape
Launcher icon used as round icon did not have a circular shape
Launcher icon used as round icon did not have a circular shape
Launcher icon used as round icon did not have a circular shape
Launcher icon used as round icon did not have a circular shape

The image `ic_launcher.png` varies significantly in its density-independent (dip) size across the various density versions: mipmap-hdpi/ic_launcher.png: 683x683 dp (1024x1024 px), mipmap-mdpi/ic_launcher.png: 1024x1024 dp (1024x1024 px), mipmap-xhdpi/ic_launcher.png: 512x512 dp (1024x1024 px), mipmap-xxhdpi/ic_launcher.png: 341x341 dp (1024x1024 px), mipmap-xxxhdpi/ic_launcher.png: 256x256 dp (1024x1024 px)

The image `ic_launcher_round.png` varies significantly in its density-independent (dip) size across the various density versions: mipmap-hdpi/ic_launcher_round.png: 683x683 dp (1024x1024 px), mipmap-mdpi/ic_launcher_round.png: 1024x1024 dp (1024x1024 px), mipmap-xhdpi/ic_launcher_round.png: 512x512 dp (1024x1024 px), mipmap-xxhdpi/ic_launcher_round.png: 341x341 dp (1024x1024 px), mipmap-xxxhdpi/ic_launcher_round.png: 256x256 dp (1024x1024 px)

The following unrelated icon files have identical contents: ic_launcher.png, ic_launcher_round.png, ic_launcher.png, ic_launcher_round.png, ic_launcher.png, ic_launcher_round.png, ic_launcher.png, ic_launcher_round.png, ic_launcher.png, ic_launcher_round.png
Misleading file extension; named `.png` but the file format is `JPEG`

The standard Android way to capitalize cancel is "Cancel" (tip: use `@android:string/cancel` instead)
Use the KTX extension function `SharedPreferences.edit` instead?
Use the KTX extension function `String.toUri` instead?

Use the KTX extension function `Bitmap.scale` instead?
issue.errorLine1: val scaled = if (scale > 1f) Bitmap.createScaledBitmap(oriented, newW, newH, true) else oriented

issue.message: Use the KTX extension function `Bitmap.scale` instead?
issue.errorLine1: val scaled = if (scale > 1f) Bitmap.createScaledBitmap(oriented, newW, newH, true) else oriented
